hard.next_step: Confidence 50% 18th May - Met the HOP/Champion (Abraham, - Bilal the EB was unavailable) - he confirmed we are shortlisted against one other provider, their current thinking is that all non-essential spend is paused until later in the year whilst the conflict/blockade continues (new construction projects are paused, though they have several years of projects underway) - their current concern is the materials prices have sky-rocketed due to the blockade & fuel prices - I have planted some seeds around my blog post on investing in procuretech to protect their margins & unlock better materials pricing), offered to present to upper mgt and/or bring Carl in in helpful to show how we can help strengthen their economics, he will talk to Bilal. 16th April - Project continues to be on pause due to the war, but expecting to resume, confirmed they had shortlisted two vendors and Zycus is one, (wouldn't say who else) - current expectation is that they will resume in Q2 - moving back to end Q2. 25th Mar - Response that the project is paused due to the war priorities: " Given the present conditions, we have a delay in the closure of this project. We will provide further updates should there be any changes." appears we are still in the mix as one of their options. 13th Mar - Reached out again for an update (several out of office responses) and requested a meeting after the Eid break. 17th Feb - Same response, not interested in meeting up... "Thank you for your patience, the project is still on and under review with our consultant . The team will reach out to you as & when there are new developments". 16th Feb - Gone quiet since they refocussed on a reduced scope, escalated to senior EB requesting a f2f this week, reducing confidence to 5%. 16th Jan - Pushing to get a meeting while Tanya is in town end Jan/early Feb, Abraham will get back to us. 20th Dec - Pushed a couple of times for updates - response 'management are evaluating, will update in the new year' 19th Nov - As expected, when they provided the usage for the pilot it's too low, rather than walk away I have added some modules they were interested in and pitched 2 scenarios as our 'min prepared to support', still a small deal but could be acceptable, let's see their response, if negative its dead. Dropped confidence to 5%. 30th Oct - Commercial discussion held, changes in approach, now looking to run a pilot approach to a business unit before deploying to wider business , reduces the scope and also changed sourcing to a later phase - now on the cusp of being too small, will get clarity once there are eInvoicing estimates of usage - moved close date back to Q4 and confidence down to 10% 28th Oct - Call scheduled Thurs 30th to discuss commercial estimation. 22nd Oct - Nudged to see if they need help with completing the commercial estimation together. 14th Oct - POC went well, they are asking for pricing, have shared the estimation template and offer to run through it together. 13th Oct - POC with Bilal (Exec Sponsor) confirmed onsite scheduled for 14th Oct (with Tanya & Dan onsite) 7th Oct - POC held onsite with Khansaheb senior manager (Abraham) and Crowe consultants, IF successful they will escalate to another session with Bilal (Exec sponsor) 23rd Sept - Pre-POC meeting set for 29th to review POC prior to live demo session (later that week TBC). 11th Sept - POC details shared to run a live demo .... pilots team tied up for next few weeks Tanya will have to build delaying the process. 25th Aug - No response from Ravi, called and postponed (also not clear Abraham is back yet) - some option offered, no response yet. 14th Aug - Ravi scheduled 26th but we have a clash, requested to reschedule. 7th Aug - Demo'ed to Bilal (GM) and exec sponsor, went well, very engaged - next step to build a POC demo based on their processes when Abrahim returns on 26th (maybe run it on 10th when Tanya/Carl are in Dubai? TBC). 31st July - Ravi called to say that the GM (Bilal) doesn't want to wait for Abraham's response - demo being arranged for next Weds-Fri. 29th July - Presented to Abraham (HOP), went well, feedback was positive, next steps are: follow up demo on invoicing & Sourcing, to sign an NDA, to get a proposal and present to management prior to a decision, shared the pricing estimate template and NDA underway. - moved to Formal Evaluation. 22nd July - Following demo request had a disco call with their consultant (Ravi - Crowe UAE) managing the project, interested in S2C but priority focus is supplier management- next step is to present to head of procurement, will be presenting iSupplier/ZSN, iRisk and iSource- scheduled for 29th Jul20
hard.ais_why:
• Per the 16 Apr 2026 rep note from Dan Quinn: 'Project continues to be on pause due to the war, but expecting to resume, confirmed they had shortlisted two vendors and Zycus is one... current expectation is that they will resume in Q2.' Per 17 Feb 2026: 'Not interested in meeting up... "Thank you for your patience, the project is still on and under review with our consultant. The team will reach out to you as & when there are new developments".' Abraham Mathew is shortlisting Zycus but is not engaged on any AI conversation.
• Per the 19 Nov 2025 rep note: 'As expected, when they provided the usage for the pilot it's too low, rather than walk away I have added some modules they were interested in and pitched 2 scenarios as our "min prepared to support", still a small deal but could be acceptable.' Product scope is iSource;iSupplier;eInvoicing;iRisk - foundational only, no Merlin/Agentic/ANA. Avoma meetings (9 total, khansaheb.com domain) focus on Crowe-led RFP mechanics, not AI.
• High confidence: 9 valid Avoma meetings on Khansaheb domain, products in scope have no AI component, BD = Unengaged, project on pause due to war. Multiple rep notes spanning Nov 2025-Apr 2026 contain zero references to Merlin/Agentic/AI from any named Khansaheb stakeholder. Clear AI Resistant.

north_star_verdict.headline: POC-validated deal frozen for roughly 8 months by an external non-essential-spend pause: champion Abraham Mathew re-engaged in person on 18 May 2026 but sits in 'wait and watch' mode, economic buyer Bilal Hamadeh has not joined a working session since the Oct 2025 POC, scope has shrunk to a single-entity iSupplier pilot, and the account was just handed to a new Regional Vice President on 29 Jun 2026.
north_star_verdict.math: Close date 2026-08-31 is 56 days out (today 2026-07-06). Shortlisted since 17 Apr 2026; first reached Formal Evaluation 29 Jul 2025, so roughly 11.5 months in cycle. Last genuine two-way buyer touch was Abraham Mathew's 19 May 2026 'wait and watch' email (about 7 weeks ago); every touch since (Horizon invite 5 Jun, new-RVP introduction 3 Jul) is rep-side. With budget frozen and no restart date, the remaining path (spend unfreeze, management approval, contracting, deployment) cannot complete by 31 Aug 2026, so the date is not credible.
evidence: Next Step 18 May 2026: 'all non-essential spend is paused until later in the year whilst the conflict/blockade continues, new construction projects are paused'.
Buyer email 19 May 2026 (Abraham Mathew): 'we are on a wait and watch mode. Give it some time, I will get back to you on the way forward.'
Next Step 16 April 2026: 'confirmed they had shortlisted two vendors and Zycus is one (wouldn't say who else)'.
Field history 29 Jun 2026: Owner changed Dan Quinn to Mohamad Alhakim (Regional Vice President); new owner emailed Abraham 3 Jul 2026 introducing himself as primary contact.

Forecast is Best Case but the pilot budget is unapproved and frozen and the economic buyer has not joined a working session since Oct 2025, so Best Case is not defensible; honest category is Pipeline.

metrics.narrative: Value drivers are articulated qualitatively but never quantified. The Oct 2025 POC and 30 Oct 2025 commercial call confirmed scope across supplier onboarding, eInvoicing (AI OCR capture and 3-way matching) and Merlin intake, targeting automation of manual vendor onboarding and invoice processing. Since the spend freeze the live metric is margin protection: per Next Step 18 May 2026 the buyer's stated concern is that 'materials prices have sky-rocketed due to the blockade and fuel prices', and Dan planted the procuretech-to-protect-margins angle. No cost-per-invoice, cycle-time or savings figure has been agreed, so building a quantified ROI case Abraham can carry to management is the open move that unfreezes budget.

economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is Bilal Hamadeh, logged in the Next Step history as GM and Executive Sponsor, with pilot spend requiring management approval above him. Bilal engaged early (demo 7 Aug 2025 'very engaged'; onsite POC 13-14 Oct 2025) but has not joined a working session since Oct 2025 and was explicitly unavailable for the 14 and 18 May 2026 meetings. On the 30 Oct 2025 call the Crowe consultant Ravi Shankar confirmed the authority chain: 'Ultimately it goes to Bilal, and Bilal will approach management for this pilot project.' Budget sits with Bilal and management and is frozen; the deal is single-threaded to Abraham who cannot release it, so re-engaging Bilal on restart timing is the priority. His Salesforce contact record carries no title (NA), a hygiene gap versus the rep-logged GM/Exec Sponsor role.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are well documented from the demo and POC cycle: a vendor portal with self-registration plus KYC/compliance document validation (trade-license, VAT and ICV certificate checks with expiry alerts), configurable role-based access control, 3-way invoice matching, AI OCR invoice capture, and phased ERP integration. On the 14 Oct 2025 in-person demo Tanya walked the confirmed Khansaheb onboarding flow (vendor registration form, duplicate check, conditional approvals, risk assessment) as 'absolutely in scope'. eInvoicing adds a hard regulatory criterion raised by the relevant stakeholder and Abraham on 30 Oct 2025: compliance with the UAE FTA e-invoicing 5-corner model routed through a government-approved intermediary, plus minimal government-portal integration failure ('there will be failure, but it should be minimal').

decision_process.narrative: Partner-led, phased evaluation read from the call sequence: discovery with Crowe consultant Ravi Shankar (22 Jul 2025), Head-of-Procurement demo to Abraham (29 Jul), GM/Exec-Sponsor demo to Bilal (7 Aug), configuration review (30 Sep), onsite POC with Bilal and Crowe (7-14 Oct), then commercials (30 Oct). On 30 Oct 2025 the buyer reshaped into a phased delivery, Phase 1 being an iSupplier and eInvoicing pilot on a limited set of entities with sourcing deferred to 'a different project', explicitly pending management review through Bilal. Ravi Shankar stated 'every pilot project goes to management for review and approval'. The process is now stalled at management review pending an external, date-uncertain spend-freeze decision.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are partly surfaced. On 29 Jul 2025 next steps included signing an NDA, receiving a proposal and presenting to management, with the NDA 'underway'. Commercial structure is clear from 30 Oct 2025: minimum three-year term (typical five) because SaaS cost is front-loaded, bundle discounts only if iSourcing is taken as an immediate Phase 2 rather than a separate 'different project', and pricing dependent on confirmed user counts (two admin, five power, one business user) and invoice volumes. A regulatory dependency gates the eInvoicing paper: it must integrate with the UAE FTA portal via a pre-approved intermediary, and Dan committed Zycus would be contractually compliant with the new regulation. No redlining, MSA or signature stage has been reached because budget is frozen; visibility returns only when management approves the unfreeze.

identify_pain.narrative: Confirmed operational pain: manual vendor onboarding with compliance-document validation (trade-license, VAT and ICV certificate expiry) across multiple entities running on different ERPs, manual invoice processing, and no real-time supplier visibility. On 30 Oct 2025 Abraham and Ravi Shankar detailed the multi-entity, multi-ERP complexity ('the divisions run on different ERPs, the books are different') and government-portal integration failures they want minimized. The overlay pain since the blockade is financial: spiking materials and fuel prices squeezing margins (Next Step 18 May 2026). Pain owner is Abraham for operations; the margin and economics concern is owned by Bilal and management, which is exactly where the value case must land.
sources: Avoma commercial call 30 Oct 2025 (Abraham Mathew, Ravi Shankar)

champion.narrative: Abraham Mathew (Head of Procurement) is a developing champion: he led the project from the first demo (29 Jul 2025), drove detailed POC configuration feedback (30 Sep and 30 Oct 2025), agreed to share sample vendor documents, and met Dan face-to-face on 18 May 2026 when no other contact would. His advocacy is currently constrained: his 19 May 2026 email was honest but passive ('wait and watch, I will get back to you'), he lacks authority to unfreeze budget, and the deal is single-threaded to him. What has developed is consistent access and hosting; what is still missing is a secured internal Bilal/management meeting for Zycus and a quantified case he can sell upward. The 29 Jun 2026 owner handover to a new RVP now risks the personal continuity Dan built with him.

competition.narrative: Zycus is one of two shortlisted vendors (Next Step 16 April 2026, 'wouldn't say who else'), but the rival's identity has never been disclosed, so it cannot be directly counter-positioned. The real live competitor is do-nothing / deferral: the buyer has paused all non-essential spend and frames procurement automation as discretionary while the conflict continues. No active bake-off is running while spend is frozen, so the unnamed peer is a dormant secondary threat. The wedge is to reframe procuretech as a margin-protection lever during the downturn (Dan's blog and Agentic AI summit content shared May 2026) backed by the validated POC; surfacing the rival's identity via Crowe consultant Ravi Shankar is an opportunistic, lower-priority move.

deal_movement.summary: The only movement in the last 90 days is administrative and rep-side: on 29 Jun 2026 the account was reassigned from Dan Quinn to a new Regional Vice President, Mohamad Alhakim, who introduced himself to Abraham on 3 Jul 2026; the amount was trued up to 150,000 on 30 Jun 2026. The buyer itself has not moved since the 18-19 May 2026 in-person meeting and 'wait and watch' email; the project remains frozen by the external non-essential-spend pause.

items.change: Account reassigned from Dan Quinn to new Regional Vice President Mohamad Alhakim; new owner emailed Abraham introducing himself as primary contact
Buyer confirmed 'wait and watch mode, give it some time, I will get back to you on the way forward'
In-person meeting with Abraham (Bilal unavailable): confirmed shortlisted against one other vendor, non-essential spend paused, materials prices spiking
Close date pushed 30 May to 31 Aug 2026, stage moved Formal Evaluation to Shortlisted, forecast raised to Best Case

competitive_position.summary: Zycus is one of two shortlisted vendors (16 April 2026) but the live battle is against do-nothing / deferral: an external non-essential-spend freeze from the conflict/blockade has paused the project with no restart date. The single strongest current threat is that spend pause; the undisclosed peer vendor is a secondary, dormant threat because no active bake-off runs while spend is frozen. Win path: reframe procuretech as downturn margin protection, arm Abraham with a quantified case, and convert the validated POC into management approval once spend unfreezes.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Functional fit is strong and validated on the confirmed pilot scope (supplier onboarding and eInvoicing); the exposure is commercial, not product: Zycus repeatedly flagged the shrunken pilot may be 'too small', and eInvoicing carries an unresolved UAE FTA regulatory dependency.

best_practice_check.summary: Three win-strategy levers matter now: protect relationship continuity through the owner handover, break the single-thread to reach the budget-holder, and convert a shrunken pilot into a defensible margin-protection case.
flags: Owner just changed from the relevant stakeholder to a new RVP on 29 Jun 2026 mid-freeze: the new owner must re-personalise the Abraham relationship fast before the warm continuity Dan built decays.
Deal is single-threaded to Abraham, a developing champion with no budget authority: multi-thread to economic buyer Bilal Hamadeh and the management layer that approves pilot spend.
Scope has collapsed to a sub-$150k single-entity iSupplier pilot: reframe from 'small pilot' to a quantified margin-protection business case so the deal survives the do-nothing / too-small risk.

champion_strength.summary: Abraham Mathew is a consistent, engaged champion who hosts the rep and drives feedback, but is currently passive ('wait and watch'), lacks budget authority, and has not yet secured an internal management meeting for Zycus; the 29 Jun 2026 owner handover adds continuity risk.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AIS is 2.0 / Negative and the buyer actively deferred the AI surface: on 30 Oct 2025 Merlin intake was parked ('pause this one for the time being, I don't see if we would go forward') and the confirmed scope is foundational (iSupplier, eInvoicing) with no Merlin/Agentic component. The resistance is buyer-driven, not a positioning miss; an AI-led narrative is not the wedge here, margin protection is.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score 2.0, AIS_Status Negative. Calls corroborate the field: the buyer deferred Merlin intake and autonomous sourcing, kept scope to foundational supplier onboarding and eInvoicing, and no named Khansaheb stakeholder engaged on any AI conversation across Nov 2025-Jul 2026. Genuinely AI-resistant, not under-rated.

scope_change.from: Group-wide Source-to-Pay vision across Khansaheb (iSourcing plus eInvoicing plus supplier management, ~50%+ of the group)
scope_change.to: Single-entity iSupplier pilot on roughly 30-40% of the group, eInvoicing possible in phase one, sourcing deferred to a separate later 'different project'
scope_change.detail: On 30 Oct 2025 the buyer reshaped the deal into a small phased pilot to prove value before selling the success story to the group; Dan repeatedly warned it may be too small to pursue and dropped confidence, a defensive cost-cutting / phased-implementation signal reinforced by the later non-essential-spend freeze.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: POC-validated but frozen: Zycus is one of two shortlisted vendors, but Khansaheb has paused all non-essential spend due to the conflict/blockade with no restart date, scope has shrunk to a small iSupplier pilot, the economic buyer has gone quiet since Oct 2025, and the account was just handed to a new RVP mid-freeze.

win_position.text: Why the low number: Shortlisted would normally cap confidence near 70, but this earns far less because budget is frozen with no restart date, so there is no live buying process to win right now.
Zycus is one of two shortlisted vendors after an onsite POC Abraham Mathew received positively (14 Oct 2025), so we remain a credible finalist once spend unfreezes.

Scope has shrunk from a group-wide Source-to-Pay vision to a single-entity iSupplier pilot Dan repeatedly warned 'may be too small for us' (30 Oct 2025), a defensive signal that drags the win read down.

deal_momentum.text: No buyer-facing working session since the 14 Oct 2025 POC; the last two-way exchange was Abraham's 19 May 2026 'wait and watch' email, and everything since is rep-side (Horizon invite 5 Jun, new-RVP intro 3 Jul).
The close date slipped from 30 May to 31 Aug 2026 on 17 Apr 2026 with no buyer-confirmed restart, so momentum is flat-to-negative.

customer_commitment.text: Commitment is passive: Abraham confirmed the shortlist and hosts the rep but is in 'wait and watch' mode and cannot release budget; Bilal must take any pilot to management for approval (Ravi Shankar, 30 Oct 2025).
The buyer did invest in a full onsite POC and a detailed commercial-scoping call, so latent intent exists once the external spend freeze lifts.

deal_risk.text: An external non-essential-spend freeze from the regional conflict/blockade has paused all discretionary spend with no restart date (18 May 2026), a do-nothing / deferral risk rather than a loss to a competitor.
Ownership changed from Dan Quinn to a new RVP on 29 Jun 2026 mid-freeze, risking loss of the personal relationship continuity Dan built with Abraham.

deal_momentum.evidence: No buyer working session since 14 Oct 2025; last buyer reply 19 May 2026 'wait and watch'; close date slipped to 31 Aug 2026.

customer_commitment.evidence: Passive champion with no budget authority; pilot frozen and must go to management for approval.

deal_risk.evidence: Non-essential spend freeze with no restart date; scope shrunk to a small pilot; owner changed mid-freeze.

critical_signals.text: The live fight is against do-nothing, not a rival: Khansaheb froze all non-essential spend during the conflict and there is no active bake-off with the one undisclosed shortlisted vendor.
Scope has collapsed to a sub-150k single-entity iSupplier pilot that the rep repeatedly flagged may be too small to pursue, and the pilot budget remains unapproved and frozen.
The last real buyer contact was Abraham's 19 May 2026 'wait and watch' reply after an in-person meeting; the budget-holder Bilal has stayed out since the Oct 2025 POC.
A new Regional Vice President, Mohamad Alhakim, took the account on 29 Jun 2026 and has only just emailed Abraham to introduce himself, so the buyer relationship is mid-handover.

customer_preference.evidence: Shortlisted as one of two vendors and the POC went well, but the most recent buyer posture is 'wait and watch mode, give it some time', with no Zycus-specific next step requested since Oct 2025.

business_case.evidence: Strong qualitative pain (manual onboarding, multi-ERP complexity, margin squeeze from spiking materials/fuel prices) but no ROI, savings, cost-per-invoice or cycle-time figure has been quantified or agreed.

contributions.evidence: scope narrowed vs prior — On 30 Oct 2025 the buyer reshaped the deal into a small phased pilot to prove value before selling the success story to the group; Dan repeatedly warned it may  (buyer likely defensive on cost or phased implementation)
Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found.

commentary.win_position: Win 38 is carried by champion strength +0.30 (weight 15); exec access strength +0.30 (weight 15). Offsetting it: momentum 47 vs stage-expected 56.
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 47 (roughly flat): Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 32 is driven by Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found; Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 14 is anchored on the stage win (38), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 47, and dragged by risk 32. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 3 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, exec and champion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
